description: Eight low-risk coping strategies (answer2.pdf). Server offers at most 2 per turn for selected_strategy; IDs are fixed for analysis.

strategies.name: Delay the first drink by ~15 minutes
strategies.example_if_then_format: If I walk in wanting a drink, I'll shower first and wait 15 minutes before deciding.

strategies.name: Alternate alcohol with water
strategies.example_if_then_format: If round two starts, I'll drink a full glass of water between each alcoholic drink.

strategies.name: Keep alcohol out of sight at home
suitable_for: Visual cues on the counter or fridge
strategies.example_if_then_format: If I sit down at night, I'll put bottles in a cabinet and keep only NA options visible.

strategies.name: Plan a leave time before you go out
suitable_for: Risk rises after a certain round
strategies.example_if_then_format: If it's a work dinner, I'll plan to leave by 9pm before I arrive.

strategies.name: Text a friend before the risky stretch
strategies.example_if_then_format: If it's Friday after work, I'll text ___ 'two-minute check-in?' before the first drink.

strategies.name: Bring a non-alcoholic drink you like
strategies.example_if_then_format: If there's a party, I'll bring sparkling water so I already have a full glass.

strategies.name: Practice one short line to refuse or slow down
strategies.example_if_then_format: If someone pushes another round, I'll say 'I'm pacing tonight—still on this one.'

strategies.name: Substitute a fixed action for the first urge (walk/shower/snack/tea)
strategies.example_if_then_format: If the first urge hits, I'll walk around the block before opening anything.
